speaker: SS

speaker: KG
words: Ito take homes, make homes of 'em. And my folks at that time were living, well

words: county.

speaker: KG
words: back and forth. Oh, that was awful.

words: named ourselves and played house. Why we had fun, and oh, when the snow started

words: together and have friends in. But we didn't have much to do there.

words: time to one kid.

words: to my next door neighbor's here, she and, she asked me to come in and it showed

words: we be gone and when should we be home and things like that. They had some jurisdiction

speaker: SS
words: fathers, that mothers were more lenient. Maybe kids were afraid of their fathers.

speaker: KG
words: about the house and things like that. But sometimes^e'd go to bed at night,

speaker: KG
words: we wouldn't go to sleep, we'd be noisy and mamma would say,"Alfred, speak to

speaker: KG
words: those children." And that's all that had to be done. But it was just the same

speaker: KG
words: now, listen. When our boys were little, I'd put 'em to bed, is that thing

speaker: KG
words: registered?

speaker: SS
words: Yeah.

speaker: KG
words: And they'd want a drink and they'd want this. And finally I'd say,"This is the

speaker: KG

words: doing, and I think there was much more training in the home than there is nowadays.

words: and 33 were in school, the two older ones. When we went home from school we

words: legs or something like that for us to wear in the house. Yes, and there were

words: we made Idaho overshoes. With gunny sacks, wrapped our feet in gunny sacks and

words: went to school. Now those were called, here they are, our Idaho overshoes were

words: I showed you...

words: and cured their meat. They had smokehouses where they cured the hams and all

words: so they had smokehouses and^u^ed them til they would keep all summer. We don't

speaker: KG
words: get nice smokeri foftm like WG used to tnen« Yes il was Pretty rugged in those

words: days, I'll tell ya. But everybody in the country around up there were practically

words: in the same boat, you know, same circumstances as other people. So.

words: some way. I don't remember about that at all, but I know that there was lots

words: just well educated people, but I can't tell you where they came from, 'cause

speaker: KG
words: King.

words: It was just a community church.

words: he'd(sings)Do,me,so.(laughs)

speaker: KG
words: Each song. When he'd get the tone. And he started the singing. He led the singing.

words: long in their thirties or so, like that. She was considered a maiden lady. And

speaker: KG
words: a community church. Ho one certain religious sect.
